channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hawthorn Dominates Essendon in AFL Clash, Young Bombers Gain Experience
item.description: In a commanding display, Hawthorn outplayed Essendon in their AFL clash, leaving the Bombers struggling to keep up. The Hollywood Hawks showed excellent teamwork and goal-scoring power, with standout ...

item.title: Early Benchmarks Show Apple's MacBook Neo Outperforming Top x86 CPUs in Single-Core Tests
item.description: Benchmark results from Notebookcheck reveal that the new Apple MacBook Neo, powered by the A18 Pro chip, delivers record-breaking single-core performance that surpasses all current x86 processors from...

item.title: Controller of Budget Corrects Misattributed Sh44.5 Million Expenditure
item.description: The Controller of Budget, Margaret Nyakang'o, has clarified reports claiming that the Second Lady, Joyce Kithure, spent Sh44.5 million in the first half of the 2025/26 financial year. According to OCo...

item.title: DOJ Raises Evidence Standard in Preliminary Investigations: What You Need to Know
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) recently increased the amount of evidence prosecutors need to file a case in court, a move upheld by the Supreme Court (SC). Under Department Circular No. 15, series of...

item.title: Corporate PAC Funds Influence Key Democratic Senate Primaries
item.description: The article examines the significant role of corporate political action committee (PAC) money in the 2026 Democratic primaries for U.S. Senate seats, particularly in competitive races. Despite pledges...

item.title: eThekwini probes container-cleaning firm over toxic fumes affecting Durban school
item.description: The eThekwini municipality is investigating a container-cleaning company suspected of causing toxic fumes that affected learners and staff at Umbilo Secondary School in Wentworth, Durban, on Thursday ...

item.title: European video games containing loot boxes to be rated PEGI 16 or higher
item.description: The Pan-European Game Information (PEGI) system is introducing new age ratings for video games containing loot boxes across Europe, including the UK, from June. Games with paid random items, commonly ...

item.title: Kenya Establishes Meteorological Service Authority to Strengthen Weather Forecasting and Climate Monitoring
item.description: Kenya has officially put in place a new law to establish the Kenya Meteorological Service Authority, aimed at streamlining and regulating meteorological services across the country. President William ...

item.title: Two Indian nationals killed and ten injured in Muscat attack; five others discharged from hospital
item.description: On Friday, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) confirmed that two Indian nationals were killed and ten others sustained injuries in an attack in Muscat, Oman. The incident occurred in Sohar city, r...

item.title: Partial Closure of Globe Roundabout for Nairobi River Bridge Repairs
item.description: Motorists using Thika Superhighway should brace for some delays as Kenya National Highways Authority (KeNHA) announced a temporary partial closure at Globe Roundabout. The closure, which runs from Mar...

item.title: Ondo Governor Clarifies Stand on Court Ruling About 2028 Election
item.description: Governor Lucky Aiyedatiwa of Ondo State don react to di judgement wey Federal High Court deliver wey declare say e no fit contest for governor position again for 2028. Di court tok say Constitution no...

item.title: PM Shehbaz holds fuel prices steady to ease pressure on public despite global oil surge
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced on Friday that the government will not increase the prices of petroleum products, even though global oil prices have risen sharply due to ongoing regional tensi...

item.title: Over 20% of Australian Teens Continue Using TikTok and Snapchat Despite Age Restrictions
item.description: Two months after Australia enforced a social media ban for users under 16, data from parental control software Qustodio shows that more than one-fifth of 13-to-15-year-olds still access TikTok and Sna...

item.title: Suspended KZN police sergeant alleges assault and torture by senior officers
item.description: Samkeliso Mlotshwa, a suspended South African police sergeant, has presented serious allegations of assault and torture against KwaZulu-Natal police commissioner Lt-Gen Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi during a par...

item.title: Four US military personnel killed in Iraq plane crash as rescue continues for two survivors
item.description: Four of the six crew members aboard a US KC-135 military refuelling aircraft have been confirmed dead following a crash in western Iraq. The incident occurred during Operation Epic Fury, involving a s...

item.title: Olympic Champion Hidilyn Diaz Joins UP College of Human Kinetics as Weightlifting Instructor
item.description: Hidilyn Diaz, the Philippines' first-ever Olympic gold medalist, has officially started teaching weightlifting at the University of the Philippines College of Human Kinetics. Diaz, known for her groun...

item.title: The 2026 Burning Man Temple of the Moon: A Temporary Space for Reflection
item.description: At Burning Man 2026, the Temple of the Moon, designed by artist James Gwertzman, takes center stage as a unique architectural and spiritual structure. Inspired by the Queen of the Night cactus, which ...

item.title: Historical Perspective on MPs Switching Parties to Join the Government
item.description: Floor-crossing, where opposition Members of Parliament (MPs) switch allegiance to join the governing party, has long been a feature of Canadian politics. While seeing four MPs switch in just four mont...

item.title: Government begins expansion of Tanga Port to improve efficiency
item.description: The Tanzanian government is moving forward with a major project to expand and modernize Tanga Port to boost efficiency and revenue collection. According to Tanga Port Manager Salehe Mbega, the plan in...

item.title: Irish TD criticizes high resale prices for Six Nations rugby tickets despite anti-touting law
item.description: A Fine Gael TD from Offaly, John Clendennen, has raised serious concerns regarding the ongoing practice of ticket touting in Ireland ahead of this weekend’s Six Nations rugby match between Ireland and...
